It ranks third because the measured best surface is still the 128x128 PTX branch and grouped_rows=8 is the cleaner within-family fallback.
The upside is broader than dir_01 and dir_02, but so is the implementation surface, which is why it stays below the two PTX options.
candidates.hypothesis: The live queue still needs one geometry-side branch that does not merely reshuffle the current PTX 128x128 control path. The in-tree 256x128 hot-band kernel already exists with async staging and higher reuse, and round_history kept it among the stronger non-PTX family representatives. Promoting that kernel as the hot-band default would test whether a larger reuse tile can reduce control overhead and improve latency hiding more effectively than another local PTX micro-scheduling change. It remains third because the active PTX branch is still the measured winner and grouped_rows=8 is the stronger like-for-like fallback inside the PTX family.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Geometry-level latency hiding and control amortization on the 256x128 hot-band path, not the current 128x128 PTX wait/commit seam.
candidates.risk: Medium to high. This is a broader hot-band dispatch swap onto a non-default geometry, so it is reversible but carries more surface-change risk than the two PTX-local options above.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the 256x128 promotion cannot get materially closer to the 24.16-24.18 ms PTX band or if it opens a broad hot-band regression without counter improvements.

notes: Persistent frontier tracks 59 historical candidates; 12 family representatives are currently active for selection.
